name: Kristen Bennett
school: Department of Astronomy and Planetary Science
focus: Lunar and Martian geology • Planetary remote sensing • Surface evolution
bio: Kristen A. Bennett is a planetary scientist specializing in the geology of the Moon and Mars, with expertise in remote sensing and planetary data analysis. Her research focuses on interpreting surface features and geologic history using data from NASA spacecraft, including visible, thermal-infrared, and near-infrared observations. She has contributed to studies of Martian sedimentary deposits and lunar volcanic processes, helping scientists better understand planetary climates and surface evolution. Dr. Bennett earned her Ph.D. in Geological Sciences from Arizona State University, where she studied Martian and lunar deposits, and has worked as a postdoctoral researcher at Northern Arizona University. She later served as a research space scientist at the U.S. Geological Survey’s Astrogeology Science Center, where she contributed to planetary exploration efforts and mission science teams.
